plan_metadata.name: Feature DA Manifest File Function Path Support

steps.description: Select the "Declarative Agent – Agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot" option in the "New Project" dropdown within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "No Action" option in the dropdown menu within the "Create Declarative Agent" popup in Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Click the "Default Folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" selection dropdown at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.
Click the "Provision" option under the "LIFECYCLE" section in the left sidebar of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.

Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code – Insiders, shown while viewing the "Overview of the Declarative Agent template" README with the "Provision" step selected in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar.
Type 'notifications: Show Notifications' into the VS Code command palette input at the top of the window.
Press Enter to select the "Notifications: Show Notifications" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
@code wait for #text:/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/appPackage/build/manifest.dev.json exist. Timeout: 120s
@assertion notification "provision stage executed successfully" exist.

@code open json file #text:/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/appPackage/declarativeAgent.json, set value for "instructions" to a new value `"$[file('/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/appPackage/instruction.txt')]"`
@code open json file #text:/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/appPackage/manifest.json, set value for "version" to a new value "1.0.1"
Press F1 to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code while the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension panel is active.
Type text 'Notifications: Clear All Notifications' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field at the top center of the interface.
Press Enter to select "Notifications: Clear All Notifications" from the command palette dropdown in Visual Studio Code.
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code, while the "MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT" sidebar is active.
Type 'PROVISION' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field to search for the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision" command.
Press Enter to select the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision" command in the VS Code command palette.
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code while the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension panel is active.
Type 'NOTIFICATIONS: SHOW NOTIFICATIONS' into the VS Code Command Palette input at the top center of the window.
Press Enter to select the "Notifications: Show Notifications" command from the Visual Studio Code command palette.
@assertion There is a notification showing provision is successfully executed.
Click the "MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT" panel icon in the Visual Studio Code sidebar to expand or focus the toolkit panel.
Click the "appPackage" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to expand its contents.
Click the editable text field for renaming the "build" folder in the File Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "declarativeAgent.dev.json" file in the build folder of the Explorer panel in Visual Studio Code to select or open it.
Click on the text editor area in Visual Studio Code, specifically within the "declarativeAgent.dev.json" file at the start of the instructions string, to position the cursor for editing.
@assertion the value of instructions is starting with "You are a declarative agent"
@code open json file #text:/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/appPackage/declarativeAgent.json, set value for "instructions" to a new value `"$[file('instruction.json')]"`
Press F1 to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code while editing declarativeAgent.dev.json.
Type 'ZIP APP PACKAGE' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field at the top center of the window.
Press Enter to select the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Zip App Package" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Press Enter to confirm selection of "manifest.json" in the "Select Teams manifest.json File" dialog within Visual Studio Code.
Press Enter to select the "dev" environment from the "Select an environment" dropdown in Visual Studio Code.
@assertion There is an error with "invalid file" in the notification.
Press the F1 key in Visual Studio Code to open the Command Palette, while viewing declarativeAgent.dev.json in the editor.
Type “>notifications: clear all notifications” into the VS Code command palette input field to trigger the "Notifications: Clear All Notifications" command.
Press Enter to select "Notifications: Clear All Notifications" from the VS Code command palette dropdown.
Press Enter to select "Notifications: Clear All Notifications" from the VS Code command palette dropdown.
@code open json file #text:/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/appPackage/declarativeAgent.json, set value for "instructions" to a new value `"$[file('color.png')]"`
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code while editing the "declarativeAgent.dev.json" file within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace.
Type 'ZIP APP PACKAGE' into the Visual Studio Code command palette at the top of the Microsoft 365 Agents extension interface.
Press Enter to execute the "Zip App Package" command from the command palette in Visual Studio Code.
Press the Enter key to confirm the selection of "manifest.json" in the Select Teams manifest_json File dialog within Visual Studio Code.
Press Enter to select the "dev" environment from the "Select an environment" dropdown in Visual Studio Code.
@assertion There is an error with "UnsupportedFileFormat" in the notification.
